image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: No good news for Indian B1/B2 visa applicants yet: $750 fast visa appointment pilot program starts in Mexico
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 1,800-year-old Roman camp linked to Marcus Aurelius found in Slovakia
item.description: Archaeologists have discovered an approximately 1,800-year-old Roman military camp in southwestern Slovakia that may have been used by Emperor Marcus Aurelius' army during the Marcomannic Wars. The site contains skeletons believed to be Roman soldiers buried in shallow graves, ditches and wells, along with weapons, armour, coins and military sandals. Researchers say the rare discovery could provide new insights into Roman military campaigns, although years of scientific analysis are needed to answer key questions about the soldiers' deaths and the camp's abandonment.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Setback for Hindu Americans: California court strikes down state’s Diwali holiday law
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 500-year-old place with 200 skeletons buried beneath it: Construction workers uncover it
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'My partner is half-Indian': Elon Musk rejects racism allegations, says his son's name honours Indian scientist Subrahmanyan Chandrasekhar
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Oregon proposes fees of up to nearly $5 million for undersea cables using its coastal seabed, with payments benefiting public schools
item.description: Oregon is introducing new fees for undersea cable installations near its coast. These charges will generate revenue for the state's public K-12 schools. Existing fees were established about twenty-five years ago and were much lower. The new rates aim to attract tech investment while ensuring compensation. This update follows a 2020 incident involving a cable construction project.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Resilient shelter dog Duke finds forever home after heartbreaking journey
item.description: Duke, a shelter dog, faced multiple losses including his home and bonded sister. He endured two failed adoptions within a single day at the shelter. A volunteer shared Duke's story online, which reached a woman nine hours away. This woman instantly decided to adopt Duke, bringing him to his new home. Duke now lives a happy life with a family committed to him.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Canada recalls Briine Pacific Tide sparkling water in Alberta after 355ml cans begin bulging
item.description: The Government of Canada has recalled Briine Pacific Tide Sparkling Spring Waterin Alberta after some355 ml canswere found to be bulging. The recall has been issued due tonon-harmful microbial contamination (quality or spoilage). Consumers and retailers have been advised not to use, sell, serve or distribute the affected product and to check thetwo affected best-before codes: 01AL2028 and 02AL2028.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Dozens of Indian-origin men among 21 charged in $139m US-Canada cross-border drug bust
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Ireland has recovered the remains of 99 infants from the former Tuam mother and baby home; DNA analysis may help restore some of their identities
item.description: Ireland has recovered ninety-nine infant remains from a former mother and baby home. Forensic excavations are ongoing at the Tuam site, revealing hidden burial chambers. DNA analysis aims to identify the children and restore their identities to families. This effort acknowledges a dark chapter in the country's institutional history. The state is now confronting its past and treating the deceased with dignity.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Spain invokes first wildfire emergency as fires rage across Europe; over 10,000 evacuated
item.description: Spain declared a national emergency due to widespread wildfires. France requested European Union assistance for a major blaze near Bordeaux. Tens of thousands were evacuated from affected tourist and residential areas. This crisis highlights the intensifying impact of climate change on southern Europe. The region is experiencing record-breaking heat and drought conditions.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Myles Garrett lists $2.1M Ohio home after Rams move—here’s what stands out
item.description: Star defensive end Myles Garrett is selling his Ohio residence. This move follows his significant trade to the Los Angeles Rams. The Wadsworth home, purchased in 2018, is now listed for sale. It features five bedrooms and five-and-a-half bathrooms across two acres. Garrett's departure marks a new chapter on the West Coast.
item.dc:creator: TOI Lifestyle Desk

item.title: Japanese companies making toilets, glass fibre and seasonings, not AI chips, are among the AI boom's biggest winners
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Vietnam moves to bar under-16s from posting, commenting on social media
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: German farmer saves 'gay' sheep from slaughter and uses proceeds to help people flee homophobic persecution
item.description: A German farmer rescues gay sheep, which are often discarded by the agricultural industry. His Rainbow Wool project transforms these animals into symbols for animal and human rights. Profits from wool sales and collaborations support the LSVD+ foundation, aiding refugees. This initiative highlights the naturalness of homosexuality and challenges societal prejudices. The project continues its vital work, advocating for acceptance and safety globally.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: NATO tests giant grass to restore Ukraine's war-damaged soil; may break down explosive residue
item.description: Scientists in the Czech Republic are testing giant Miscanthus, a fast-growing perennial grass, to see if it can help restore Ukraine's war-damaged farmland. Backed by a NATO project, the research aims to remove pollutants from contaminated soil, improve soil health and eventually make the land suitable for farming again while producing biomass for renewable energy.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists left 'Moon cement' outside the ISS for six months; it came back up to 35% stronger
item.description: A pioneering development in space materials, the recently created 'Moon cement' derived from lunar and Martian simulants has shown exceptional durability, surviving for half a year outside the International Space Station unscathed. Remarkably, one formulation achieved a 35% boost in compressive strength over Earth-based counterparts, illuminating promising avenues for constructing habitats on the Moon utilizing local soil resources.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: NYC Upper West Side stabbing: Zohran Mamdani reveals disturbing details of 'hateful' attack
item.description: New York City police are investigating two recent stabbings on Manhattan's Upper West Side. Witnesses reported the suspect shouted "Allahu Akbar" during both assaults on victims. Authorities have arrested 51-year-old Raul Morales in connection with these separate incidents. Investigators are examining mental health factors and whether these attacks qualify as hate crimes.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
